explicit_text: I was born in South Korea in 1960, and my early life there was very difficult. My grandmother worked as a prostitute to support the family, sometimes feeding us red clay just to ease our hunger. Because of these harsh conditions, my mother made the painful decision to give me and my younger brother up for adoption. South Korea is where my story began, and it shaped much of my early experiences before I came to America.

That country is where my story began, and it shaped much of my early experiences before I came to America.

explicit_text: At the age of 10, in 1970, I was adopted by an American family in Minnesota. I remember arriving at the Minneapolis airport feeling scared and confused, not knowing any English except a few simple words. Minnesota was where I began my new life, learning English with the help of a tutor and adjusting to American customs. It was a tough transition, but Minnesota became my new home and the place where I started to heal from my past.

explicit_text: When I was 17, I reconnected with my birth mother in Burlington, New Jersey. This meeting was a significant moment in my life, bridging the gap between my past in South Korea and my present in the United States. Burlington became the place where I faced the emotional complexities of my adoption and learned more about my origins. It was a bittersweet reunion that helped me understand my identity better.

explicit_text: Since coming to the United States, my life has been a journey of adaptation and growth. I was adopted here and faced many challenges learning the language and customs, especially as a young immigrant. Over the years, I have worked in various roles, including missionary work in Thailand and China, and now I run my own custom design business while working at a candle warehouse. The United States has been both a place of struggle and opportunity for me.

explicit_text: I spent several years doing missionary work in Thailand after settling in the United States. This experience allowed me to connect with different cultures and give back in meaningful ways. Thailand was a place where I could extend my faith and service beyond my own story, enriching my life and broadening my perspective. My time there was an important chapter in my journey as an immigrant and a missionary.
